exclusion_rules.description: Only bona fide residents of Manipur are eligible. The scheme targets poor and needy families registered under it.

exclusion_rules.condition: is_bpl == false and not (in_secc or aay_card or is_widow or is_disabled or in_notified_category)
exclusion_rules.description: Eligibility is limited to defined categories: families identified in SECC, BPL/Antyodaya (AAY) card holders, widows, disabled persons, and notified groups (Anganwadi workers/helpers, ASHA workers, Ima Market women vendors, newspaper hawkers/local media). Households outside these categories are not covered.

family_criteria.family_definition: Eligible family registered under the scheme and issued a CMHT card with a unique family identification number (UID). Coverage is a family floater shared across registered members.

covered_procedures: Cashless treatment for 7 critical illness groups at government and empanelled private hospitals
General hospitalization treatment up to Rs 50,000 per family per year (sub-limit within the cover)
Transport allowance of Rs 300 per hospital visit (annual cap Rs 3,000)
Out-of-state treatment daily allowance of Rs 1,000 per hospitalization day (up to Rs 10,000)
excluded_procedures: Treatment costs above the applicable annual family ceiling

required_documents.name: Identity Proof (Aadhaar / Voter ID / PAN / Driving Licence / Passport)
required_documents.local_name_hi: पहचान प्रमाण (आधार / वोटर आईडी / पैन / ड्राइविंग लाइसेंस / पासपोर्ट)
required_documents.notes: Any one government photo identity document for the beneficiary and family members.

required_documents.name: AAY (Antyodaya Anna Yojana) Card
required_documents.notes: Required where eligibility is claimed via the AAY category. Widows and disabled persons provide the relevant supporting document.

required_documents.name: CMHT Registration Fee Rs 50
required_documents.notes: A Rs 50 family registration fee applies, but is waived (exempted) for AAY card holders, disabled persons, and widows.

enrollment_steps: Step 1: ASHA workers notify eligible families at least 2 days before the registration drive in the area
Step 2: Eligible beneficiaries attend the enrollment centre with identity proof and any category document (AAY card, widow/disability proof)
Step 3: Documents are verified by the enrollment team
Step 4: Pay the Rs 50 family registration fee (waived for AAY holders, disabled persons, and widows)
Step 5: A CMHT card with a unique family identification number (UID) is issued
Step 6: For treatment, visit a government or empanelled private hospital with the CMHT card and ID proof; the health coordinator facilitates cashless admission after Insurance/ISA approval
Step 7: Claim the Rs 300 per-visit transport allowance and, for out-of-state referrals, the Rs 1,000/day allowance as applicable
description_for_embedding: Chief Minister-gi Hakshelgi Tengbang (CMHT) is Manipur's flagship state health assurance scheme providing cashless treatment to poor and needy families at government hospitals, health centres, and empanelled private hospitals. It provides up to Rs 5 lakh per family per year for seven critical illness groups — cardiovascular diseases, kidney ailments, neurological disorders, liver diseases, cancer, burns, and neonatal disorders — with a sub-limit of Rs 50,000 per family per year for general hospitalization treatment. (The scheme launched in January 2018 with a Rs 2 lakh cap, since enhanced; the current cover should be reconfirmed on the official portal before relying on the figure.) Eligibility is category-based rather than income-based: families identified in the Socio-Economic Caste Census (SECC), Antyodaya Anna Yojana (AAY) card holders, widows, disabled persons, and notified groups such as Anganwadi workers and helpers, ASHA workers, Ima Market women vendors, and newspaper hawkers/local media persons. A nominal Rs 50 family registration fee applies but is waived for AAY card holders, widows, and disabled persons. ASHA workers notify eligible families ahead of registration drives; on verification a CMHT card with a unique family identification number (UID) is issued. The scheme also pays a transport allowance of Rs 300 per hospital visit (capped at Rs 3,000 a year) and, for medically referred out-of-state treatment, a daily allowance of Rs 1,000 per hospitalization day up to Rs 10,000. Required documents include any government photo ID (Aadhaar, Voter ID, PAN, driving licence, or passport) and the relevant category document. Portal cmhtmanipur.gov.in.
